predicted_external_diagram.situation: Alex feels stuck in a monotonous routine with work and complicated family dynamics.
predicted_external_diagram.automatic_thought: I’m just not good enough; I’m a constant letdown.
predicted_external_diagram.behavior: Alex goes through the motions at work, avoids social situations, and sits around feeling disconnected.

ground_truth_external_diagram.situation: Alex's cousin invited him to attend his upcoming wedding.
ground_truth_external_diagram.automatic_thought: It will be stressful and negative; people will ask me questions I cannot answer or don't want to answer; my mum would be ashamed of me and critical of me as always; They don't want me there anyway, nobody likes me in this family.
ground_truth_external_diagram.emotion: anxious, worried, fearful, scared, tense; sad, down, lonely, unhappy
ground_truth_external_diagram.behavior: Ignored the invitation and did not respond to the RSVP request. Ignored phone calls from family.

predicted_internal_diagram.relevant_history: The client reports feeling a sense of monotony and lack of fulfillment in their daily routine, with work being unexciting and lacking hobbies. They mention complicated family dynamics and feeling pressure from family expectations, leading to feelings of falling short and disappointment. The client experiences a heavy weight of expectations and feels stuck in a loop, unable to see a way out. They express fear of drifting through life and missing out on meaningful experiences, feeling disconnected and isolated from friends, and struggling with anxiety and self-doubt in social situations.
predicted_internal_diagram.core_beliefs: I am not good enough | I am a constant letdown | I am not interesting enough | I am not really part of the group
predicted_internal_diagram.intermediate_beliefs: If I can't meet their expectations, then I am a letdown. | The client believes that reaching out might bother others or that they won't care. | If I try something and it doesn’t work, then I might make things worse. | If I avoid social situations, then I feel more isolated.
predicted_internal_diagram.coping_strategies: The client copes by avoiding social situations to prevent feeling overwhelmed, although this leads to feelings of disconnection and isolation. They also engage in overthinking and second-guessing themselves after social interactions. The client considers setting time limits for overthinking and using distractions like books or shows to manage anxiety. They also contemplate using reminders of their strengths and positive intentions to counteract doubts.

ground_truth_internal_diagram.relevant_history: The patient has a history of substance abuse and has been through rehab to overcome it. He has had issues with his family, particularly with his mother, where he has felt rejected and emotionally neglected. He has struggled with obesity since childhood, which has affected his self-esteem and body image. He also has a history of being victimized and bullied due to his weight.
ground_truth_internal_diagram.core_beliefs: I am trapped. | I am out of control. | I am unlovable. | I am undesirable, unwanted.
ground_truth_internal_diagram.intermediate_beliefs:
Helpless: I'm just not very good at handling stress and I have poor self-control, which is why I need to not put myself in stressful situations. 
 Unlovable: If I show my true self, people will reject me the way my mother rejected me.
Depressive variant: Helpless: There's nothing I can do to change my situation. I cannot control myself. 
Unlovable: I don't deserve to be happy, so why even try to stay clean?
ground_truth_internal_diagram.coping_strategies: The patient has adopted avoidance as a coping strategy by distancing himself from his family to reduce exposure to negativity and conflict. He has started scheduling pleasant activities and planning his day ahead of time in order to maintain a sense of control over his emotions and circumstances.
